skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
12/m − 3/p = −3/q

The given equation relates the variables m, p, and q, where m > 0, p > 0, and q > p. Which expression is equivalent to m?
rationale: Choice C is correct. Rearranging: 12/m = 3/p − 3/q = 3(q − p)/(pq). Inverting: m/12 = pq/(3(q − p)), so m = 12pq/(3(q − p)) = 4pq/(q − p). Choices A, B, and D arise from algebraic errors (failing to combine fractions over a common denominator, sign error, or inverting incorrectly).
